GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Why The Bold Type fills the gap in our hearts left behind by Sex and the City", "Since its initial release, The Bold Type has been compared to Sex and the City, The Devil Wears Prada, Gossip Girl and even Broad City, but those who watch the show will know that the glamorous antics of the Scarlet Magazine crew remain totally unique. Far from drawing comparisons, what makes The Bold Type so utterly watchable is that it has a piece of all of our favourite all-female television heroes in it–from the realism of living in New York presented by Abbi and Ilana in Broad City, to the fashion legacy left behind by Gossip Girl. If you're not familiar with the show, let us back up a bit. The Bold Type, which is available exclusively on Stan, follows the lives of three modern young women who work at Scarlet, a fashion magazine in New York City. We see the girls navigate everything from friendship and relationship drama to the #MeToo movement, but the fashion envy stays constant. So, grab the rosГ© and meet us in the fashion closet, as we unpack what makes the show our favourite fashion series to date. Are you a Kat, Jane or Sutton? Like Sex and the City, the best part about The Bold Type is that there's a spectrum of different women that we can relate to and look up to, only modernised for a new audience. The question of whether you're a Carrie, Miranda, Charlotte or Samantha has turned into whether you're a Kat, Jane or Sutton. Jane Sloan is a hard-hitting journalist who isn't afraid to push the envelope to succeed; Kat Edison is the social media director who lifts up fellow women of colour and promotes their incredible work; and Sutton Brady is a fashion assistant who has overcome a testing childhood and major career change to flourish at the magazine.The Bold Type is imbued with more diversity than previous shows of its genre and its heroines are more relevant for a new generation of women. It celebrates true female friendships in all their mess and glory. These friends don't just gather once a week for brunch to talk about men–they share their entire lives whether it be supporting one another through heartbreak, fighting self-doubt or dealing with medical scares. They fight and reconcile and always put each other first. It's an honest representation of female friendships and the complex emotions that go along with them. In the latest season, we watch Jane struggle with recent news of her fertility issues that becomes overshadowed by an unfaithful boyfriend, Kat continue to explore her sexuality, and Sutton consider changing her entire life trajectory. It's this depiction of real life, on the sidelines of the glitz and glamour, that the showrunners are bravely wading into. What's an all-female squad without a fearless leader to look up to? Jacqueline Carlyle is the inimitable editor-in-chief of Scarlet, portrayed by iconic American actor Melora Hardin. She's an inspiring yet relatable woman who doesn't favour the bullshit and doesn't adhere to traditional gender roles. She's part Professor McGonagall, part Enid Frick with a dash of Miranda Priestly's style. Even with her role on the line, she continues to fight for women and inspire others to do the same. The fashion is better than ever before. Let's face it, the real reason The Bold Type fills the gap left behind by some of our other favourite fashion dramas is the style. Kat, Jane and Sutton evolve their unique personal style every season. And those regular meetings in the fashion closet which offer sneak-peeks of new season Gucci will never lose their impact. The brand new season of The Bold Type is now streaming, with new episodes dropping same day as the US, only on Stan. Start your 30 day free trial now."
